identity.name: Kimpton Marlowe Hotel
identity.address: 25 Edwin H. Land Blvd, Cambridge, MA 02141

discovery.text: Recommended via Concierge Chat for Boston late-summer trip

narrative.summary: Quirky Kimpton boutique on the Charles River with great views and easy access to Harvard and MIT energy. Pets welcome, bikes to borrow, and a rooftop terrace that makes the most of summer evenings.

blocks.title: The Place
blocks.body: The Marlowe sits right on the Charles River in Cambridge, between Harvard and MIT territory. It's got the Kimpton DNA — deliberately quirky, wine hour every evening, bikes to borrow, pets welcome — but the Charles River views and proximity to the academic world give it a distinctly Cambridge character. The rooftop terrace in late August is excellent.

blocks.title: Location
blocks.body: Cambridge puts you in the college-town energy John was asking about — Harvard Square is minutes away, MIT a short walk. Red Line into downtown Boston is easy. This is the base for someone who wants intellectual atmosphere, river views, and to drift between Kendall Square's restaurants and the Yard.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Early September when students are back — Harvard Yard fills up, the restaurants around Porter and Harvard Squares get that buzzing semester energy, and the weather is still warm enough for the rooftop.
